title: Quotient vector spaces
text:
Let \(V\) be a vector space and \(W \leq V\) a subspace.
Then the quotient \(V/W\) is the set of cosets [ v + W. ]


Take (V = ^2) and (W = (e_1)).
Then (V/W) is one-dimensional.

title: Home - Welcome!
text:
I’m building a math and personal website with Obsidian, Quarto, and GitHub Pages.
I am a math student interested in geometry, topology, dynamical systems, and machine learning.
Here is an explanation of my interests for a mathematical audience or for a lay audience.

title: Home - Welcome!
text:
Mathematical audience
In sum, I interested in the following themes and areas, especially their interface.

Topology, geometry, and moduli
Equivalence, invariance, and functoriality
Distance, approximation, and stability
Deformation, perturbation, and singularity
Dynamics, evolution, and qualitative theory
Information, inference, and complexity

title: Home - Welcome!
text:
Lay audience
I enjoy math about shapes, change, and prediction. Or more specifically

Shapes, spaces, and patterns
Change, motion, and transformation
Similarity, learning, and complexity
